		<post-id xmlns="com-wordpress:feed-additions:1">24278914</post-id>	</item>
		<item>
		<title>Accelerating Sustainability With Smart Manufacturing</title>
		<link>https://semiengineering.com/accelerating-sustainability-with-smart-manufacturing/</link>
					<comments>https://semiengineering.com/accelerating-sustainability-with-smart-manufacturing/#respond</comments>
		
		<dc:creator><![CDATA[Mark da Silva]]></dc:creator>
		<pubDate>Thu, 16 Jul 2026 07:01:16 +0000</pubDate>
				<category><![CDATA[Blogs - MD]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Connect With SEMI]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Manufacturing, Packaging and Materials]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Industry 4.0]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Micron Technology]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[SEMI]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[semiconductor manufacturing]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[smart manufacturing]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[sustainability]]></category>
		<category><![CDATA[Ulvac]]></category>
		<guid isPermaLink="false">https://semiengineering.com/?p=24278947</guid>

					<description><![CDATA[<p>An Industry 4.0 technology roadmap for semiconductor device makers that covers carbon emissions, water, and hazardous waste.</p>
<p>The post <a href="https://semiengineering.com/accelerating-sustainability-with-smart-manufacturing/">Accelerating Sustainability With Smart Manufacturing</a> appeared first on <a href="https://semiengineering.com">Semiconductor Engineering</a>.</p>
]]></description>
										<content:encoded><![CDATA[<p><strong>By Brian J. Coppa, Amit Srivastava, Mark da Silva, and Anshu Bahadur</strong></p>
<p>The <a href="https://www.semi.org/en/industry-groups/smart-manufacturing">SEMI Smart Manufacturing Initiative</a> is a global effort focused on leveraging the most advanced technology to enhance productivity of electronics manufacturing facilities. Among the key technology advances this group seeks to promote is the value of implementing Industry 4.0/5.0 technology to deliver increased return-on-investment (ROI). A recent white paper published by the initiative focused on the productivity improvements that can be achieved by the formation of digital twins in semiconductor plants [1]; however, sustainability was not the focus, providing an opportunity for future pre-competitive work.</p>
<p>To address this, the SEMI Smart Manufacturing Initiative formed the &#8220;Accelerating Sustainability with Smart Manufacturing&#8221; <a href="https://www.semi.org/en/industry-groups/smart-manufacturing/industry-4-driven-sustainability">task force</a> in 2023 to benchmark industry best practices that enable manufacturing facilities to meet their sustainability goals faster. The roadmap would complement the <a href="https://www.semi.org/en/industry-groups/sustainability">SEMI Sustainability Initiative</a> by following a bottom-up approach to identifying the industry’s best practices. The task force primarily focused on fab device production, which is widely seen as the largest driver of the microelectronics industry’s Scope 1–2 emissions, water use and hazardous waste consumption, versus other branches of the supply chain.</p>
<p>This task force developed a comprehensive solutions-based Scope 1 (process-based, direct) and Scope 2 (energy-based, indirect) emissions roadmap, incorporating the Connecting, Sensing and Predicting pillars developed by the Smart Manufacturing Initiative [2], and applied them at cleanroom, subfab and facilities levels for brownfield device-making facilities. While these phases are meant to be cumulative for maximum impact on targeted sustainability metrics, the SEMI task force understands that not every fab that applies the roadmap as a tool for sustainability purposes will advance use cases all the way to the Predicting phase. Moreover, special use cases are defined for greenfield fabs in a separate section of the roadmap, covering new infrastructure and more disruptive changes to existing operations to guide streamlined deployment.</p>
<p>These key features are the foundation used to develop the first half of the roadmap for addressing Scope 1 and 2 emissions, leveraging Industry 4.0/5.0 technology, and published as a collaborative SEMI <a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-with-semi-smart-manufacturing-whitepaper-registration.html">white paper</a> [2]. The second half of the roadmap presented at SEMICON West 2025 [3] is covered in this latest <a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-smart-manufacturing-ai-roadmap-for-device-makers-part2-download-form.html">white paper</a> [4] and follows the same methodology though focused on reducing water consumption and hazardous waste in device-making fabs.</p>
<p><a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-smart-manufacturing-ai-roadmap-for-device-makers-part2-download-form.html"><img data-recalc-dims="1" loading="lazy" decoding="async" class="alignnone size-full wp-image-24278950" src="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=400%2C400&#038;ssl=1" alt="" width="400" height="400" srcset="https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?w=400&amp;ssl=1 400w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=300%2C300&amp;ssl=1 300w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=150%2C150&amp;ssl=1 150w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=100%2C100&amp;ssl=1 100w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=24%2C24&amp;ssl=1 24w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=48%2C48&amp;ssl=1 48w, https://i0.wp.com/semiengineering.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/SEMI_Accelerating-Sustainability-with-Smart-Manufacturing-fig1.webp?resize=96%2C96&amp;ssl=1 96w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 400px) 100vw, 400px" /></a></p>
<p>Altogether, both SEMI white papers comprise the first comprehensive semiconductor industry roadmap covering carbon emissions, water and hazardous waste, while emphasizing the benefits of smart technology. The roadmap numerically rates each innovative use case in terms of relative impact on reducing a fab baseline void of smart elements and based on technology readiness level (TRL) with respect to future production capability.</p>
<p>This functional roadmap will be available soon to the industry as a customizable model, denoted as the SEMI Smart Sustainability Model (SSM), wherein users can estimate sustainability gains by applying some or all listed use cases based on the nature of the facility’s sustainability profile and goals (i.e. relative proportion of water consumption sources at all levels of their operation, by adjusting weighted impact factors in the model). A base case assessment of proportional resource consumption representing an industry average 300mm fab, based on published data and task force estimates, is provided for fabs without detailed tracking to constitute the SEMI Smart Sustainability Roadmap.</p>
<p>Many sustainability organizations across the semiconductor industry are focused on problem-based assessments, which often highlight water scarcity or total industry carbon footprint growth, and this can lead to IDMs, foundries, OSATs and others in the supply chain tackling sustainability from an isolated, project-based perspective. Moreover, a top-down approach to sustainability in fabs does not scale as well as a bottom-up approach to counteract the issue of increasing device process complexity and the larger associated process flow, requiring a higher amount of carbon emissions, water and hazardous waste, which needs to be addressed. For instance, a purchase power agreement for renewable energy is determined based on a finite amount of expected fab energy usage. However, energy-efficiency aided by AI across all levels of the fab is much more scalable, as a fab expands or additional process steps are added per product. Therefore, a data-driven approach, tracking emissions, water, and waste and linking technologies to future targets, is most effective, making this roadmap unique in scope and approach. A unified fab-wide data platform leveraging digital twins can further improve outcomes by linking water and waste metrics with downstream KPIs like recycling rates.</p>
<p>The roadmap’s best practices can apply to any device-making fab, as it relies on Industry 4.0/5.0 technologies rather than compromising process flows as in alternative fab sustainability models. ROI benefits identified include cost-savings on process materials, utilities, regulatory, and labor plus higher yield and shorter cycle time after implementation. In summary, the use cases within the customizable model are quantified based on normalized impact to the current baseline level so that device-makers can benchmark themselves and prioritize investment in the most effective technologies to meet their sustainability goals.</p>
<p>For future updates on the SEMI Smart Manufacturing Initiative, including the upcoming SSM product release, please visit the Initiative&#8217;s <a href="https://www.semi.org/en/industry-groups/smart-manufacturing">website</a>. To learn more about the roadmap, download prior white papers:</p>
<ul>
<li>White Paper 1: <a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-with-semi-smart-manufacturing-whitepaper-registration.html">Accelerating Sustainability with SEMI Smart Manufacturing</a></li>
<li>White Paper 2: <a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-smart-manufacturing-ai-roadmap-for-device-makers-part2-download-form.html">Accelerating Sustainability with SEMI Smart Manufacturing: AI Roadmap for Device Makers Part II</a></li>
</ul>
<p><strong>References:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>M. da Silva and K. Somani, &#8220;Digital Twins in Semiconductor Manufacturing,&#8221; SEMI, Milpitas, CA, 2024.</li>
<li>B. Coppa, A. Srivastava and M. da Silva, &#8220;Accelerating Sustainability with Smart Manufacturing: Roadmap for Device Makers,&#8221; SEMI white paper (Available <a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-with-semi-smart-manufacturing-whitepaper-registration.html">here</a>) &#8211; November 2024.</li>
<li>B. Coppa, A. Srivastava, “SEMI Smart Sustainability Roadmap Part II: AI Blueprint for Device Makers,” at SEMICON West, October 2025.</li>
<li>B. Coppa &amp; A. Srivastava, “Accelerating Sustainability with Smart Manufacturing: AI Roadmap for Device Makers Part II” SEMI white paper (Available <a href="https://discover.semi.org/accelerating-sustainability-smart-manufacturing-ai-roadmap-for-device-makers-part2-download-form.html?_gl=1*c5jaz7*_ga*MTc4MTMyOTIxLjE3NzYxOTM0Nzg.*_ga_QY5ZZS97GM*czE3ODEwNDA1Nzgkbzk3JGcxJHQxNzgxMDQwNTgxJGo1NyRsMCRoMTkwMDk1OTA4NQ..">here</a>) – May 2026</li>
</ol>
<p><em>Brian J. Coppa is product engineering lead at ULVAC. </em></p>
<p><em>Amit Srivastava is staff program manager for Smart Manufacturing &amp; AI at Micron. </em></p>
<p><em>Anshu Bahadur is senior program manager, Technology Coalitions at SEMI. </em></p>
<p>The post <a href="https://semiengineering.com/accelerating-sustainability-with-smart-manufacturing/">Accelerating Sustainability With Smart Manufacturing</a> appeared first on <a href="https://semiengineering.com">Semiconductor Engineering</a>.</p>
]]></content:encoded>

					<description><![CDATA[<p>PCIe ATS verification; physical AI; adopting AI EDA; custom AMBA Viz plugins; V2G; fab construction workforce.</p>
<p>The post <a href="https://semiengineering.com/blog-review-july-15-3/">Blog Review: July 15</a> appeared first on <a href="https://semiengineering.com">Semiconductor Engineering</a>.</p>
]]></description>
										<content:encoded><![CDATA[<p>Cadence&#8217;s Anupriya K explains how <a href="https://community.cadence.com/cadence_blogs_8/b/fv/posts/demystify-address-translation-services-ats-in-pcie-6-0">Address Translation Services</a> (ATS) acts as the fast lane for PCIe memory access by caching address translations directly on the device and what makes it one of the toughest verification problems to solve.</p>
<p>Synopsys&#8217; Greg Sorber considers the economic potential of <a href="https://www.synopsys.com/blogs/chip-design/physical-ai-silicon-software-robots.html">physical AI</a> and the impact that embedding intelligence into robots, vehicles, and equipment will have on future factories, hospitals, and city streets.</p>
<p>Siemens&#8217; Emma-Jane Crozier provides some tips for getting started with using <a href="https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/cicv/2026/07/08/ai-in-eda-practical-advice-no-matter-where-you-are-on-the-journey/">AI in EDA</a>, including identifying the biggest pain points for your team, adopting it with a specific flow or goal in mind first, working closely with the vendor, and measuring the impact to share with other teams.</p>
<p>Arm&#8217;s Tony Nip shares how to create <a href="https://developer.arm.com/community/arm-community-blogs/b/soc-design-and-simulation-blog/posts/create-your-own-amba-viz-plugins">AMBA Viz plugins</a> with custom hardware signal, bus, and event analysis for more flexible RTL debug and performance analysis.</p>
<p>Keysight&#8217;s Andrew Cifala finds that achieving the promise of <a href="https://www.keysight.com/blogs/en/inds/interoperability-key-to-v2g-acceleration">vehicle-to-grid</a> technology will require end-to-end interoperability and a shared test environment across utilities, EV OEMs, charging providers, and other stakeholders.</p>
<p>Skanska&#8217;s Joycelyn Yue cautions that semiconductor fab buildout could be hampered by a shortage of qualified <a href="https://www.semi.org/en/blogs/powering-the-semiconductor-construction-boom-through-workforce-collaboration">construction tradespeople</a>, particularly in critical disciplines like electrical and mechanical work, where there&#8217;s a generational gap in the workforce.</p>
<p>Plus, don&#8217;t miss the blogs featured in the latest <a href="https://semiengineering.com/newsletter/low-power-high-performance-july-2026/">Low Power-High Performance newsletter</a>:</p>
<p>Siemens EDA’s Emma-Jane Crozier illustrates the challenges facing <a href="https://semiengineering.com/the-architecture-decisions-behind-a-production-ready-eda-ai-agent/">AI agents</a> in solving specific problems in the context of generic frameworks.</p>
<p>Rambus’ Paul Karazuba explains how <a href="https://semiengineering.com/the-expansion-of-lpddr-into-edge-ai-platforms/">LPDDR characteristics</a> once primarily associated with extending smartphone battery life map directly onto the needs of real-time on-device inference.</p>
<p>Cadence’s Sandeep Grover digs into <a href="https://semiengineering.com/ualink-under-the-hood-why-full-stack-verification-wins/">UALink verification</a> and what that entails.</p>
<p>Arm’s Satadal Bhattacharjee explains why <a href="https://semiengineering.com/from-host-node-to-heterogeneous-rack-rethinking-the-ai-cpu/">CPUs are needed</a> for agentic AI workloads.</p>
<p>Synopsys’ Sumit Vishwakarma outlines how <a href="https://semiengineering.com/cloud-hpc-for-ai-addressing-latency-cost-and-scale-at-the-architectural-level/">low-latency fabrics</a>, topology-aware scheduling, and tiered memory bring compute closer to data and reduce coordination overhead.</p>
<p>Quadric’s Marc Meszaros shows the benefits of <a href="https://semiengineering.com/benchmarking-an-npu-at-scale/">recompiling and re-profiling</a> the entire model zoo across a broad sweep of hardware configurations.</p>
<p>The post <a href="https://semiengineering.com/blog-review-july-15-3/">Blog Review: July 15</a> appeared first on <a href="https://semiengineering.com">Semiconductor Engineering</a>.</p>

																			<content:encoded>
								<![CDATA[<p>TSMC is increasing its 2026 capital budget to as much as $64 billion and adding $100 billion to its existing investment in the U.S. as it chases soaring demand from AI customers.</p><p>That demand from chip</p>]]>
